_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 GA state legislators (priority batch). Every TRUE/FALSE cites a source URL; null where no cited evidence; never inferred from party. Several individual GA roll calls are JS-blocked to automated fetch, so unconfirmed votes are nulled, not guessed. Strictly party-line marquee votes attributed per runbook rule.

profile.background: Statesboro CPA (Dabbs, Hickman, Hill & Cannon); Georgia Southern '74. State Senator SD-4 (Bulloch/Candler/Effingham/Evans/part Chatham) since a 2020 special election; chairs Senate Education & Youth. Reelected 2024; running again in the May 19, 2026 GOP primary.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored 2026-06 from GA Life Alliance pro-life certification, LegiScan roll calls, and the Georgia Legislative Freedom Index 2024 (67%). Some individual roll calls unretrievable (JS-blocked) and left null.

billy-hickman@GA.notes_append: 2026-06 evidence pass: GLA Pro-Life Certified 2020 (questionnaire: abortion illegal, no exceptions). YES SB140 (2023 minors gender-transition ban), SB233 (2024 Promise Scholarship vouchers), HB1105 (2024 anti-sanctuary), HB1018 (2024 2nd Amendment Protection Act). Freedom Index 2024 = 67%. No cross-party deviation found.

0.note: GA Life Alliance pro-life certification + questionnaire: abortion should be illegal with no exceptions (life-from-conception stance).

2.note: Voted YES on SB140 (2023), banning gender-transition procedures for minors.

0.note: Voted YES on SB233 (2024) Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers / school choice.
Voted YES on HB1018 (2024) Second Amendment Protection Act, a state measure resisting federal gun regulation.

2.note: HB1018 (2024) resists federal overreach on firearms at the state level.
Voted YES on HB1105 (2024) Criminal Alien Track and Report Act (anti-sanctuary, ICE cooperation).

profile.background: Dalton restaurant/business owner; UGA Finance '01. State Rep HD-4 (Whitfield) since 2017. A relatively moderate Republican: voted against the 2023 voucher bill and repeatedly sponsored in-state-tuition bills for DACA students. Reelected 2024; faces a GOP primary challenger May 19, 2026.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored 2026-06 from the Georgia Legislative Freedom Index, LegiScan roll calls, and news coverage. Three documented cross-party deviations (DACA tuition sponsorship; 2023 voucher NO; IVF-rights NO). Individual votes that were JS-blocked left null.

kasey-carpenter@GA.notes_append: 2026-06 evidence pass: YES SB319 (2022 constitutional carry), SB140 (2023 minors gender ban), HB1084 (2022 divisive-concepts/sports), HB1178 (2022 Parents' Bill of Rights), SB233 (2024 vouchers, after voting NO in 2023). DEVIATIONS: sponsored DACA in-state-tuition bills HB120/HB131; voted NO on 2023 SB233 vouchers (16 rural R defectors); voted NO on HB428 (2025) codifying a right to IVF (pro-life-aligned read). No GRTL/NRA numeric rating located.

2.note: Voted NO on HB428 (2025) codifying a right to IVF, consistent with opposing IVF embryo practices.
Voted YES on SB140 (2023, minors gender-transition ban) and HB1084 (2022).

4.note: Voted YES on HB1084 (2022) barring transgender athletes from opposite-sex school sports.

0.note: Voted YES on SB233 (2024) Promise Scholarship vouchers on final passage. Note: voted NO on the 2023 version (cross-party defection); 2024 YES is the operative vote.

1.note: Voted YES on HB1178 (2022) Parents' Bill of Rights.

2.note: Voted YES on HB1084 (2022) restricting 'divisive concepts' (CRT-style) instruction in schools.

1.note: Repeatedly sponsored in-state tuition for DACA students (HB120 2021, HB131 2023-24) — contrary to deportation/benefit-restriction position for those who entered as minors. Cross-party deviation.

0.note: Voted YES on SB319 (2022) constitutional/permitless carry.

profile.background: Calhoun courier-business owner; former Calhoun City Council and Board of Education member. State Rep HD-5 (Gordon/Murray/Floyd) since a 2019 special-election runoff. Reelected 2024; running again in the May 19, 2026 GOP primary.
profile.confidence_note: Thin evidence pass 2026-06: GA's per-member roll-call portals (LegiScan/Vote Smart) block automated retrieval, so only strictly party-line marquee votes are attributed; all other cells left null rather than guessed. Not on the GA Life Alliance 2024 certification list.

matt-barton@GA.notes_append: 2026-06 evidence pass (thin): individual roll calls were not separately retrievable. Attributed only strictly party-line marquee House votes per scorecard rule: SB140 (2023 minors gender ban, party-line GOP) and SB63 (2024 cash-bail, party-line). Not listed on GA Life Alliance 2024 cert. No cross-party deviation found, but absence reflects unretrievable roll calls, not confirmed party-line voting.

2.note: SB140 (2023, minors gender-transition ban) passed the House on a party-line GOP vote; Barton (R, seated) attributed to the recorded GOP side.

1.note: SB63 (2024) expanding cash-bail requirements / restricting bail funds passed the House on a party-line vote; Barton attributed to the recorded GOP side.

profile.background: Bangladeshi-American Democrat; first Muslim and first immigrant in the Georgia Senate. SD-5 (Gwinnett) since 2019; member of the legislature's LGBT caucus. Reelected 2024; running again 2026. UGA business graduate.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored 2026-06. Documented progressive positions (SB202 NO; LGBT caucus member; opposes CRT-instruction limits) are recorded as FALSE on the matching conservative-rubric cells; no conservative-aligned position found, so no credit cells. Several individual roll calls were JS-blocked and left null rather than inferred from party.

sheikh-rahman@GA.notes_append: 2026-06 evidence pass: voted NO on SB202 (2021 Election Integrity Act) — recorded roll call. Member of the GA legislative LGBT caucus (own campaign site). Platform opposes limits on race/diversity instruction. No documented conservative-side vote or statement found. No cross-party deviation. Individual votes on SB319/SB140/SB233/SB63/HB1105 not retrievable to a URL — left null.

4.note: Member of the Georgia legislature's LGBT caucus (his own campaign site) — affirms/promotes LGBTQ identity in policy, contrary to the rubric position.

2.note: Platform opposes efforts to limit teaching on race and diversity (i.e., opposed CRT-style instruction restrictions like HB1084).

1.note: Voted NO on SB202 (2021), whose signature provisions tightened photo voter ID for absentee ballots; platform opposes 'restrictive voting measures.'

3.note: Voted NO on SB202 (2021), which restricted drop boxes/mail-in access; platform supports expanded mail/early voting.

profile.background: Murray County farmer/realtor and former community banker; founded 4 Oaks Farm. State Rep HD-6 (Murray + parts of Whitfield/Gordon) since 2017. Lead sponsor of HB1045 (2024) requiring parental consent before K-12 sexual-orientation/gender-identity instruction. Reelected 2024; running again in 2026.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-scored 2026-06 from his HB1045 lead sponsorship, an on-record self-reported heartbeat-bill vote, and the Club for Growth Foundation 2024 GA scorecard (58%). Several individual roll calls were JS-blocked and left null.

jason-ridley@GA.notes_append: 2026-06 evidence pass: lead sponsor of HB1045 (2024 SOGI parental-consent / anti-secrecy bill). Self-reported voting for the LIFE Act/heartbeat bill (HB481, which recognizes fetal personhood) and 'steadfast opponent of abortion' (own newspaper statement). Club for Growth Foundation 2024 = 58%. No cross-party deviation. Individual votes on SB233/SB63/HB1105/SB202/SB319 not retrievable to a URL — left null.

0.note: On record stating he voted for the LIFE Act/heartbeat bill (HB481, which recognizes fetal personhood) and is a 'steadfast opponent of abortion.'

2.note: Lead sponsor of HB1045 (2024) restricting gender-identity instruction and barring staff from helping students hide gender identity from parents.

4.note: HB1045 (2024) opposes promotion of LGBTQ/SOGI identity content in K-12 schools.

1.note: HB1045 (2024) requires written parental consent before SOGI instruction and bars concealment of gender identity from parents.

2.note: HB1045 (2024) restricts SOGI/gender-ideology instruction in K-12.
